scenarios.title: Child Pulled from Pool Unconscious

transcript.text: 911, what is your emergency?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Tulong! May batang nalunod dito sa pool sa Sports Complex! Nakuha na namin siya pero hindi humihinga!
Is the child on a flat, firm surface?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, andito sa tabi ng pool. Ano pong gagawin namin? Bubugahan ba namin ng hangin?

options.text: Check for a pulse and start chest compressions immediately.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Immediate CPR is vital for drowning victims to circulate oxygen.

options.text: Give rescue breaths if you are trained to do so.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Drowning is a respiratory event; oxygenation is key.

options.text: Tilt the head back and lift the chin to open the airway.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Ensures the airway is clear for any spontaneous breathing.

options.text: Hold the child upside down to drain the water from the lungs.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This is dangerous and delays life-saving CPR.

options.text: Perform the Heimlich maneuver to get the water out.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Abdominal thrusts are for choking, not drowning, and can cause vomiting.

options.text: Wait for the ambulance before touching the victim.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Every second without oxygen increases the risk of brain damage.

scenarios.title: Grass fire near a Gas Station

transcript.text: 911, specify the location of the fire.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Dito sa Sambat! May sunog sa bakanteng lote, malapit na sa gasolinahan!
Is the fire moving toward the fuel tanks?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, mahangin kasi! Baka sumabog yung station!

options.text: Dispatch multiple fire engines and notify the gas station manager.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Large-scale response is needed for high-risk targets.

options.text: Instruct the gas station to activate their emergency shut-off valves.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Cutting off fuel flow prevents a massive explosion.

options.text: Evacuate everyone within a 500-foot radius.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Safety perimeters protect against potential blast zones.

options.text: Tell the caller to use a fire extinguisher on the grass fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: A portable extinguisher is insufficient for a spreading brush fire.

options.text: Instruct the caller to drive their car closer to block the fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Vehicles are flammable and should be moved away, not closer.

options.text: Advise the station to continue serving customers until the fire is closer.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Any spark during refueling could lead to disaster.

scenarios.title: Aggressive Individual in a Government Office

transcript.text: 911, keep your voice low. What is happening?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: (Whispering) May lalaki po dito sa loob, may hawak na kutsilyo. Ayaw niyang palabasin yung mga staff!
How many people are with him? Where are you hiding?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nasa ilalim ako ng desk. Tatlo po silang hostage niya!

options.text: Advise the caller to stay silent and turn their phone to vibrate.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Noise can alert the suspect to the caller's location.

options.text: Dispatch a SWAT team and hostage negotiators immediately.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Specialized units are required for hostage situations.

options.text: Tell the caller to look for an exit only if it is completely safe.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Escape is the priority, but only if it doesn't risk discovery.

options.text: Instruct the caller to jump the suspect and take the knife.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Untrained civilians should never attempt to disarm an attacker.

options.text: Ask the caller to start negotiating with the suspect.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Amateur negotiation can provoke the suspect into violence.

options.text: Tell the caller to stand up and shout for help.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Shouting reveals the hiding spot and escalates the situation.

scenarios.title: Worker Falls from a Billboard Scaffold

transcript.text: 911, is the patient conscious?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Sir, nahulog yung kasama ko habang nagkakabit ng tarp! Mga sampung talampakan ang taas!
Is he bleeding? Is he moving his legs?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Umaaray siya, masakit daw likod niya. Bubuhatin ba namin siya para dalhin sa clinic?

options.text: Keep the patient exactly where he is and do not move him.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Moving a victim with a back injury can cause permanent paralysis.

options.text: Monitor his breathing and keep him warm.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Shock is common after a major fall.

options.text: Tell him not to move his head or neck.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Spinal stability is the priority.

options.text: Help him stand up to see if he can walk.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Walking with a spinal fracture can lead to total paralysis.

options.text: Massage his back to relieve the pain.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Massaging can worsen internal injuries or fractures.

options.text: Give him water to drink while he is lying down.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: If he goes into surgery or loses consciousness, water can cause choking.

scenarios.title: Smoke Coming from a Rice Mill

transcript.text: 911, what is the color of the smoke?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Sobrang itim po ng usok dito sa kiskisan ng palay! May amoy sunog na goma rin!
Are there any chemical drums nearby?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Meron po kaming mga fuel tank para sa makina sa likod!

options.text: Dispatch a hazardous materials (HAZMAT) unit alongside fire trucks.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Industrial fires often involve chemicals or heavy fuels.

options.text: Instruct all workers to evacuate through the designated exits.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Evacuation is the priority for large industrial structures.

options.text: Advise the caller to shut off the main fuel supply if reachable.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Stopping the fuel source prevents the fire from becoming uncontrollable.

options.text: Tell the caller to enter the smoke to find the fire source.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Black smoke is toxic and causes immediate disorientation.

options.text: Suggest using a handheld fire extinguisher on the fuel tanks.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Portable extinguishers cannot cool large fuel tanks effectively.

options.text: Tell the workers to stay inside and save the rice stocks.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Property is never worth the risk of life in a fire.

scenarios.title: Group of Drunk Men Fighting in the Street

transcript.text: 911, what is the number of participants?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Mga lima silang nag-aaway dito sa labas! May hawak silang mga bote at kahoy!
Are you in a safe place away from the window?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, pero baka madamay yung mga nakaparadang motor dito. Lalabas ba ako para awatin?

options.text: Stay inside and do not intervene in the fight.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Drunk individuals with weapons are unpredictable and dangerous.

options.text: Record details like clothing and physical descriptions from safety.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Observation helps police identify suspects later.

options.text: Lock your doors and keep your house lights on.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Visibility can deter suspects from approaching your property.

options.text: Go out and try to talk the men into stopping.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: You are likely to become a target of the violence.

options.text: Throw a bucket of water at them from your balcony.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This will likely provoke the group to attack your home.

options.text: Fire a warning shot if you have a legal firearm.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Warning shots are illegal and can hit innocent bystanders.

scenarios.title: Allergic Reaction to a Bee Sting

transcript.text: 911, is the patient having trouble breathing?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, nakagat po siya ng bubuyog sa leeg! Namamaga na yung mukha niya at hirap na siya huminga!
Does he have an Epi-Pen or allergy medication?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Wala po! Ano pong pwedeng gawin habang wala pa yung ambulance?

options.text: Loosen any clothing around the patient's neck.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Assists with breathing effort as the throat swells.

options.text: Help the patient lie flat and elevate their legs.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps maintain blood pressure if they go into shock.

options.text: Keep the patient calm and stay with them.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Anxiety can worsen respiratory distress.

options.text: Give the patient food or water to wash out the sting.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Choking risk is high if the airway is closing.

options.text: Apply a hot compress to the sting area.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Heat increases blood flow, spreading the allergen faster.

options.text: Tell the patient to go for a run to sweat out the poison.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Physical exertion accelerates the allergic reaction and heart rate.

scenarios.title: Candle set fire to a curtain during brownout

transcript.text: 911, is the fire still small enough to handle?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Sir, yung kandila kanina natumba! Nasunog yung kurtina, umabot na sa kisame!
Get everyone out. Is there an extinguisher in the house?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Wala po! Magbubuhos na lang po kami ng tubig!

options.text: Use a fire extinguisher if available and the fire is small.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Proper equipment is the safest way to fight small fires.

options.text: If the fire is on the curtain, pull the rod down if safe and stomp it.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Removing the fuel from the wall prevents vertical spread.

options.text: Get everyone out and stay outside once you leave.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Smoke and heat can overcome you in seconds.

options.text: Try to blow the fire out with your mouth.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Blowing on a fire provides more oxygen and causes it to flare up.

options.text: Hide in the bathroom and wait for the firemen.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Bathrooms offer no protection from smoke and can become traps.

options.text: Throw a heavy plastic sheet over the fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Plastic melts and feeds the fire; only use a designated fire blanket.

scenarios.title: Group Destroying Public Benches

transcript.text: 911, can you provide a description of the suspects?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May mga kabataan dito sa plaza, pinagsisira yung mga bagong upuan! May mga dalang martilyo!
How many are they? Where are they heading?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Tatlo sila, papunta silang simbahan. Haharangin ko ba sila?

options.text: Stay back and observe their direction of travel.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Tracking movement helps police intercept them safely.

options.text: Note any distinguishing marks like tattoos or hair color.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Specific details are crucial for suspect identification.

options.text: Wait for the police unit to arrive before taking any action.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Professional intervention minimizes the risk of injury.

options.text: Block their path with your car or body.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Blocking suspects with tools/weapons is dangerous.

options.text: Shout insults at them to make them angry.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Aggravating suspects may lead them to attack you or bystanders.

options.text: Attempt to grab their tools from their hands.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Disarming someone is a high-risk activity that often leads to injury.

scenarios.title: Patient having sudden numbness and slurred speech

transcript.text: 911, ask the patient to smile for you.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Sir, yung lolo ko po biglang tabingi yung mukha! Hindi rin po siya makapagsalita nang maayos!
Can he raise both of his arms?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Hindi po yung kaliwa! Ano pong gagawin namin?

options.text: Note the exact time the symptoms started.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Stroke treatments are time-dependent; the 'last known well' time is critical.

options.text: Keep the patient calm and in a comfortable position.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Reduces stress on the cardiovascular system.

options.text: Prepare a list of the patient's current medications.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps EMTs and doctors provide safe treatment.

options.text: Give the patient aspirin or other medicine.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: If the stroke is a bleed (hemorrhagic), aspirin will make it worse.

options.text: Give the patient something to eat or drink.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Strokes often affect swallowing; giving food/drink can cause aspiration.

options.text: Have the patient try to exercise to 'wake up' the numb limb.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Exertion increases blood pressure and can worsen a brain bleed.

scenarios.title: Kitchen explosion in a neighborhood

transcript.text: 911, was there a loud bang?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo! Sumabog po yung kusina ng kapitbahay! May apoy at gumuhit yung dingding nila!
Is the gas still leaking? Are people hurt?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May naririnig po akong 'hissing' sound! Papasukin ko ba para ilabas yung mga tao?

options.text: Dispatch fire, ambulance, and search and rescue teams.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Explosions cause structural damage and multiple injury types.

options.text: Instruct neighbors to stay away from the building due to collapse risk.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Damaged buildings are unstable and can fall unexpectedly.

options.text: Advise against using any open flames or electronics nearby.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Gas still in the air can cause a secondary explosion.

options.text: Enter the building immediately to look for survivors.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Structural instability and gas leaks make entry lethal for civilians.

options.text: Tell the caller to try and plug the gas leak with a cloth.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Approaching an active high-pressure leak is extremely dangerous.

options.text: Instruct the caller to turn on the street lights to see better.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Flipping a light switch can trigger another explosion.

scenarios.title: Thief Caught in a Local Sari-Sari Store

transcript.text: 911, is the suspect still at the scene?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, nahuli namin sa loob ng tindahan! Pinagtutulungan na po ng mga tao dito!
Tell everyone to stop. Are there weapons involved?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Wala po siyang baril, pero galit na galit na yung mga tao!

options.text: Instruct the caller to tell the crowd to stop the physical assault.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Vigilante justice is illegal and can lead to serious legal consequences.

options.text: Advise everyone to wait for the police to take custody.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Official arrest ensures due process and safety.

options.text: Separate the suspect from the crowd if safe to do so.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents further escalation and injury.

options.text: Encourage the crowd to teach the thief a lesson.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Inciting violence is illegal and creates a liability for the dispatcher.

options.text: Tell the caller to tie the suspect to a pole in the sun.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This constitutes torture/illegal detention.

options.text: Instruct the caller to let the thief go to avoid trouble.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Letting a suspect go prevents justice and a proper investigation.

scenarios.title: Bleach Splashed into a Cleaner's Eyes

transcript.text: 911, keep the patient's eyes open. Do not let them rub.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Tulong! Nalsikan ng Zonrox yung mata ng kasama ko! Sobrang hapdi daw, hindi niya maidilat!
Is there a clean water source nearby?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, andito kami sa gripo. Bubuhusan ba namin agad?

options.text: Flush the eye with clean, lukewarm water for at least 20 minutes.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Continuous irrigation is the only way to dilute the chemical.

options.text: Ensure the water flows from the inner corner to the outer corner.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents the chemical from washing into the unaffected eye.

options.text: Remove contact lenses if the patient is wearing them.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Contacts can trap the chemical against the eye.

options.text: Apply eye drops or ointment immediately.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Ointments can trap chemicals; use only water until medical help arrives.

options.text: Rub the eye with a clean cloth to wipe the bleach out.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Rubbing causes mechanical damage to the cornea.

options.text: Use a vinegar solution to neutralize the alkaline bleach.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Never put other chemicals (even vinegar) in the eye; it causes a worse reaction.

scenarios.title: Christmas Lights Sparking in the Living Room

transcript.text: 911, is there an active flame?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Yung Christmas lights namin sa bintana, nag-spark tapos nasunog yung plastic! May apoy na!
Is the plug still in the wall?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, hindi ko mahawakan kasi may sparks! Bubuhusan ko ba ng tubig?

options.text: Turn off the main power breaker for the whole house.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Safely cuts power without the risk of touching the live socket.

options.text: Use a Class C fire extinguisher if the power is still on.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Class C extinguishers are non-conductive and safe for electrical fires.

options.text: Evacuate the house if the fire spreads to the furniture.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Life safety is more important than property.

options.text: Pour water on the sparking lights to douse the flames.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Water conducts electricity; you will get electrocuted.

options.text: Pull the burning wires with your bare hands.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Touching live, burning wires leads to severe shock and burns.

options.text: Throw a wet towel over the sparking electrical wires.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Wet towels conduct electricity and are dangerous for live wires.

scenarios.title: Jeepney Hit a Pedestrian and Fled

transcript.text: 911, what is the description of the vehicle?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May nasagasaan pong matanda dito sa Regidor! Karatula ng jeep ay 'Sta. Cruz - Siniloan', hindi huminto!
Did you see the plate number? How is the victim?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Plate number is TUV-456! Yung matanda, duguan sa kalsada! Hahabulin ko ba yung jeep?

options.text: Stay with the victim and provide basic first aid if trained.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: The victim's life is the immediate priority.

options.text: Provide the plate number and direction of travel to the dispatcher.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps other units intercept the fleeing vehicle.

options.text: Keep the scene clear for the ambulance and police.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Orderly scenes allow faster medical intervention.

options.text: Chase the jeepney at high speed to stop it yourself.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Chasing suspects causes more accidents and endangers your life.

options.text: Move the victim to the sidewalk immediately.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Moving trauma victims without a backboard can cause spinal injury.

options.text: Leave the victim to look for the driver in the neighborhood.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Never leave a trauma victim alone until professional help arrives.

scenarios.title: Man having a seizure in the Public Market

transcript.text: 911, protect the patient's head from the floor.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Dito sa palengke! May mamimili na biglang natumba at nanginginig! Bula na yung bibig niya!
Is the area clear of sharp objects?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, pero baka malunok niya yung dila niya! Maglalagay ba kami ng kahoy sa bibig?

options.text: Place something soft, like a folded jacket, under his head.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents head trauma during the seizure movements.

options.text: Turn the patient onto their side once the shaking stops.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: The 'recovery position' keeps the airway clear of saliva or vomit.

options.text: Loosen any tight clothing, especially around the neck.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Assists with breathing during and after the event.

options.text: Force an object into the mouth to protect the tongue.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This can break teeth or cause choking; people cannot swallow their tongues.

options.text: Hold the person down to stop the shaking.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Restraining someone during a seizure can lead to bone fractures.

options.text: Crowd around the person to see what is happening.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Patients need air and space when they wake up; crowds cause panic.

scenarios.title: Grass fire moving toward the Laguna Provincial Jail

transcript.text: 911, how close is the fire to the facility wall?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nasa likod na po ng jail! Malaki na yung usok, kawawa yung mga nasa loob!
Is the jail staff aware? What is the wind direction?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, nagkakagulo na sila! Papunta sa amin yung usok!

options.text: Dispatch maximum fire units and notify the jail warden.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Institutional fires require coordinated security and safety plans.

options.text: Advise the jail to prepare for internal relocation of inmates.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Moving inmates away from smoke is a priority without breaching security.

options.text: Tell neighbors to close all windows to keep smoke out.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Smoke inhalation is the primary danger for nearby residents.

options.text: Suggest the caller go help the guards with the fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Civilians should stay away from prison grounds during emergencies for security.

options.text: Open the jail gates to let the inmates out to escape the smoke.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This creates a massive security breach and public safety risk.

options.text: Advise the jail to turn off all water to save it for firemen.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: The jail needs water for its own internal fire systems and inmate hygiene.

scenarios.title: Abandoned Bag at a Busy Bus Terminal

transcript.text: 911, do not touch the bag. Describe it.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May itim na backpack dito sa ilalim ng upuan. Kanina pa po walang kumukuha, mahigit isang oras na!
Are there wires or any ticking sounds?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Hindi ko po makita, bubuksan ko ba para tignan yung ID?

options.text: Do NOT touch, move, or open the bag under any circumstances.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Suspicious packages should only be handled by bomb squads (EOD).

options.text: Evacuate the immediate area and set a perimeter.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Distance is the best protection against explosive devices.

options.text: Advise terminal security to monitor CCTV for the bag's owner.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps identify if the bag was truly abandoned or left with intent.

options.text: Open the bag to check for an address or contact info.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Opening a potential bomb can trigger the device.

options.text: Carry the bag to the police station yourself.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Moving a device can trigger it; never transport suspicious items.

options.text: Throw the bag into a trash can or out the window.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Impact can trigger explosives and trash cans focus blast energy.

scenarios.title: Diabetic Patient Acting Confused and Shaky

transcript.text: 911, is the patient conscious and able to swallow?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Yung nanay ko po, diabetic siya. Ngayon po parang tuliro siya at sobrang pawis na pawis!
Is she pale? Does she have her glucose kit?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, namumutla siya. Ano pong pwedeng ibigay na gamot?

options.text: Give her a small glass of fruit juice or soda if she is awake.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Sugar helps resolve low blood sugar (hypoglycemia) quickly.

options.text: Check her blood sugar level if a monitor is available.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Confirms the medical status for responders.

options.text: Keep her sitting down and monitor for any changes.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents falls if she loses consciousness.

options.text: Inject her with insulin immediately.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: If her sugar is already low, more insulin will kill her.

options.text: Force her to eat a large meal while she is confused.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Choking risk is high when a patient is altered; use liquids/gels instead.

options.text: Slap her face to keep her awake.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Physical assault does not fix a metabolic emergency and causes trauma.

scenarios.title: Bus Engine Fire with Many Passengers

transcript.text: 911, is the bus fully evacuated?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May apoy sa makina ng bus dito sa Gatid! Marami pong pasahero, nagkakagulo silang lumabas!
Tell the driver to open all emergency exits.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, pero may mga naiwan pang gamot sa loob! Babalikan ba nila?

options.text: Prioritize the evacuation of all passengers to a safe distance.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Buses have large fuel tanks and toxic interior materials.

options.text: Direct traffic away from the burning bus to prevent collisions.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Keeps the area clear for fire engines and prevents further injury.

options.text: Check if any passengers have mobility issues and need help.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Elderly or disabled passengers may be trapped in the panic.

options.text: Allow passengers to go back inside for their luggage.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Bus fires spread rapidly; smoke inhalation is lethal within minutes.

options.text: Tell the driver to keep the engine running to move the bus.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Running a burning engine can rupture fuel lines and cause an explosion.

options.text: Instruct the caller to use a handheld water bottle on the fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Water is useless against engine fires and puts the caller in the blast zone.

scenarios.title: Child touched an exposed wire during a flood

transcript.text: 911, what is the situation?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Tulong! Yung anak ko nahawakan yung wire sa baha, nakuryente siya! Nakadikit pa rin siya sa wire!
Do not touch the child. Is the main power switch accessible?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Hindi ko po alam! Susubukan ko siyang hilahin palayo!

options.text: Do NOT touch the victim or the water they are in.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Electricity travels through water and the human body; you will also be shocked.

options.text: Turn off the main power breaker immediately.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Cutting the power is the only way to safely stop the current.

options.text: Use a dry, non-conductive object like a wooden broom to push the wire away.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Non-conductive materials can safely break the contact.

options.text: Pull the child away with your bare hands.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: The current will pass through the child into you, causing double the casualties.

options.text: Splash water on the wire to make it spark and stop.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Water increases conductivity and will worsen the situation.

options.text: Use a metal pole to hook the wire away.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Metal is a high conductor of electricity and will electrocute the rescuer.

scenarios.title: Overturned Fuel Tanker Leaking on the Road

transcript.text: 911, stay away from the vehicle. Is there smoke?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May tumagilid na truck ng gasolinahan dito sa Bubukal! Umaagos yung gasolina sa kalsada!
Are there people smoking or using cellphones nearby?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Maraming nanonood po! May mga nakamotor din na dumadaan sa tabi!

options.text: Evacuate the area and set a 300-meter safety perimeter.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Fuel vapors can ignite from a distance; a wide perimeter is vital.

options.text: Instruct all nearby motorists to turn off their engines.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Engine heat or sparks from exhaust can ignite the fuel.

options.text: Dispatch a foam-equipped fire truck and HAZMAT team.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Standard water isn't enough; foam is needed to suppress fuel vapors.

options.text: Tell the caller to try and plug the leak with a cloth.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Direct contact with massive fuel leaks is lethal and highly flammable.

options.text: Instruct the crowd to use their phones to record for evidence.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Electronic devices can produce tiny sparks that ignite vapor clouds.

options.text: Advise the driver to try and restart the truck to move it.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Starting the engine will almost certainly ignite the leaking fuel.

scenarios.title: Elderly Woman being scammed at an ATM

transcript.text: 911, what suspicious activity do you see?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May dalawang lalaki po dito sa ATM, pilit nilang kinukuha yung card ng matanda at kinakausap siya!
Are the suspects still there? Do they have weapons?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Andito pa po, parang tinatakot nila yung lola! Haharangin ko ba sila?

options.text: Observe from a distance and take note of suspect descriptions.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Observation provides police with descriptions without risking your life.

options.text: Notify bank security or the branch manager immediately.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Bank staff can freeze accounts or lock the lobby.

options.text: Stay on the line and update the direction if they leave.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Real-time tracking is essential for a mobile unit to catch them.

options.text: Shout 'Pulis!' to scare them away.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This may cause them to hurt the victim or flee before police arrive.

options.text: Attempt to grab the elderly woman and run away.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This could cause the suspects to attack you or the victim.

options.text: Physically tackle the suspects to the ground.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: You don't know if they are armed; civilians should not use force.

scenarios.title: Victim Vomiting Blood after a Punch to the Gut

transcript.text: 911, is the victim still conscious?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May nag-away po dito, sinuntok sa tiyan yung lalaki tapos ngayon nagsusuka na siya ng dugo!
Is he pale or clammy? Lay him on his side.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, sobrang putla niya! Papainumin ko ba ng gatas para kumalma yung tiyan?

options.text: Lay the victim on their side (recovery position).
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents the victim from choking on their own blood.

options.text: Monitor breathing and pulse until EMTs arrive.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Vital signs help responders assess the level of internal trauma.

options.text: Keep the victim warm with a blanket.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents shock, which is common with internal bleeding.

options.text: Give the victim water or milk to drink.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Nothing should be given by mouth as it can cause choking or complicate surgery.

options.text: Press hard on the victim's stomach to stop the bleeding.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Applying pressure to internal bleeding can cause more organ damage.

options.text: Ask the victim to sit up and cough out the blood.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Coughing and sitting up increases internal pressure and can worsen the bleed.

scenarios.title: Solar Panel Fire on a Residential Roof

transcript.text: 911, do you see flames or just smoke?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Yung solar panel namin sa bubong, may apoy na! Ma'am, baka matupok yung buong bahay!
Do not climb the roof. Is the system connected to a battery?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo! Bubuhusan ko ba ng tubig mula sa bintana?

options.text: Evacuate the house immediately.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Solar panel fires can spread into the attic/roof structure quickly.

options.text: Switch off the solar isolation switch and the main breaker.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Isolating the power helps, though panels still generate power if there is sun.

options.text: Inform firefighters that solar panels are involved.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Solar panels remain 'live' even when switched off, posing a shock risk to firemen.

options.text: Spray water directly on the panels from a hose.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Solar panels are high-voltage; water creates a bridge for electricity to shock you.

options.text: Climb a ladder to try and smother the fire with a wet blanket.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Risk of falling and electrocution is extremely high.

options.text: Use a metal rake to pull the burning panels off the roof.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Metal conducts the electricity from the solar array directly to the user.

scenarios.title: Threatening Call received by a School Secretary
scenarios.location: Pedro Guevara Memorial National High School

transcript.text: 911, stay calm. Did the caller give a specific time?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May tumawag po sa office! Sabi may sasabog daw sa loob ng campus sa loob ng isang oras!
Did you recognize the voice? Do not use your school radio.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Hindi po! I-anunsyo ko ba sa megaphone para lumabas lahat ng estudyante?

options.text: Initiate an orderly evacuation according to the school's fire drill plan.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Orderly evacuation prevents stampedes during a high-stress threat.

options.text: Do NOT use two-way radios or cellphones near any suspicious objects.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Radio waves can inadvertently trigger certain explosive detonators.

options.text: Instruct staff to leave doors and windows open during evacuation.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Open windows can help dissipate the pressure wave if an explosion occurs.

options.text: Announce 'Bomba!' over the loud speaker to make everyone run.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Causing a panic or 'crowd crush' often results in more injuries than the threat itself.

options.text: Have the janitors go around and open all lockers to find the bomb.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Janitors are not trained for bomb disposal; suspicious items should not be disturbed.

options.text: Ignore the call as it is likely a prank for exam week.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: All threats must be treated as real until cleared by the EOD (Bomb Squad).

scenarios.title: Finger Severed by a Machine

transcript.text: 911, apply pressure to the hand. Where is the severed part?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Tulong! Naputol yung daliri ng mekaniko sa makina! Maraming dugo!
Is the bleeding controlled? Do not put the finger directly on ice.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nasa sahig po yung daliri! Ilalagay ko ba sa baso ng yelo?

options.text: Apply firm, direct pressure to the stump with a clean cloth.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Stopping blood loss is the number one priority.

options.text: Wrap the severed finger in a damp, clean gauze or cloth.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Protects the tissue from drying out without damaging it.

options.text: Place the wrapped finger in a plastic bag, then put that bag on ice.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Keeps the part cool but prevents 'frostbite' or water damage to the tissue.

options.text: Put the severed finger directly into a bucket of ice water.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Direct contact with water/ice damages the cells, making reattachment impossible.

options.text: Apply a tourniquet around the person's neck to stop bleeding.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Tourniquets are for limbs only; a neck tourniquet is lethal.

options.text: Use superglue to try and stick the finger back on.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Glue introduces toxins and prevents surgeons from properly reattaching vessels.

scenarios.title: Zero Visibility on Highway due to Rice Husk Burning

transcript.text: 911, are there any accidents already?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Hindi na po kami makakita sa highway! May nagsisindi ng 'ipa' (rice husk) sa gilid, sobrang kapal ng usok!
Turn on your hazard lights. Are you in a safe spot?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nakatigil po kami sa gitna ng kalsada, baka mabangga kami!

options.text: Dispatch a fire truck to extinguish the burning husks.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Stopping the smoke source is necessary for public safety.

options.text: Advise drivers to pull over to the far right and turn on hazard lights.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Getting off the main lane prevents rear-end collisions.

options.text: Coordinate with traffic enforcers to close that section of the highway.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Preventing more cars from entering a zero-visibility zone is crucial.

options.text: Tell the caller to speed up to get through the smoke faster.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Speeding in zero visibility leads to pile-ups and fatal crashes.

options.text: Instruct the caller to walk into the smoke to find the person responsible.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Walking onto a highway in thick smoke is a recipe for being hit by a car.

options.text: Advise motorists to use high-beam lights to see through smoke.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: High beams reflect off smoke/fog, causing more glare and less visibility.

scenarios.title: Woman being followed by a motorcycle

transcript.text: 911, do not go to your house. Is there a store nearby?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: (Panicked) Ma'am, kanina pa may sumusunod sa akin na motor. Kanina pa siya paikot-ikot!
Stay on the line. What is the color of the motorcycle?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Itim po, wala siyang plate number! Papunta na ako sa madilim na eskinita para magtago!

options.text: Go to a well-lit, crowded area like a 24/7 convenience store.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Witnesses and lights deter attackers.

options.text: Note the rider's helmet, clothing, and any bike details.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps police identify the suspect even without a plate number.

options.text: Stay on the phone with 911 until you reach a safe person/place.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Continuous contact ensures immediate dispatch if things escalate.

options.text: Run into a dark alley to hide.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Dark, isolated areas make you more vulnerable to assault.

options.text: Go directly to your home and unlock the door.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This shows the stalker where you live and makes entry easier for them.

options.text: Stop and confront the rider to ask why he is following you.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Confrontation can trigger a violent attack; never engage.

scenarios.title: Farmer bitten by a Cobra in a rice field

transcript.text: 911, keep the limb still. Can you see the snake?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nagtatrabaho po kami sa bukid, tinuklaw yung kasama ko! Nakita ko kulay brown na ahas, parang kobra!
Keep the bite area below the heart level. Is he dizzy?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo! Susubukan ko po sipsipin yung lason sa sugat!

options.text: Keep the bitten limb still and below the level of the heart.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Movement and elevation accelerate the spread of venom through the bloodstream.

options.text: Remove any rings or tight clothing near the bite.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Bites swell rapidly; jewelry can cut off circulation.

options.text: Keep the victim calm and still.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Lowering the heart rate slows the spread of venom.

options.text: Suck the venom out with your mouth.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Venom enters the bloodstream via mouth sores and does not remove the poison from the victim.

options.text: Apply a tight tourniquet above the bite.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Tourniquets trap venom in one area, often leading to amputation of that limb.

options.text: Cut the wound with a knife to let the 'bad blood' out.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Cutting causes severe bleeding and infection without removing venom.

scenarios.title: Fire in a Multi-Story Commercial Building

transcript.text: 911, what floor is the fire on?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nasa 3rd floor po kami! Usok na po yung hallway, hindi kami makababa sa hagdan!
Do not use the elevator. Go to a room with a window.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Gagamit po kami ng elevator para mabilis makababa!

options.text: Instruct the caller to stay in a room and seal the door with wet cloths.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Sealing the door prevents smoke (the main killer) from entering the room.

options.text: Tell them to stay low to the floor where the air is cleaner.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Heat and smoke rise; breathing is easier near the ground.

options.text: Advise them to signal their location using a bright cloth at the window.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps firefighters with ladders locate trapped victims quickly.

options.text: Use the elevator to escape quickly.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Elevators often fail or trap people during fires; they act as smoke chimneys.

options.text: Break the window immediately to let the smoke out.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Breaking windows can create a 'backdraft' or draw the fire toward the room.

options.text: Jump from the 3rd floor to the street.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: A 3rd-story jump often results in death or critical injury; wait for a ladder.

scenarios.title: Illegal drug use in a public restroom

transcript.text: 911, are they still inside the restroom?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May mga lalaki po dito sa banyo ng palengke, parang may hinihithit na kung ano at may mga syringe!
Do not confront them. How many individuals are there?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Tatlo sila! Bubuksan ko ba yung pinto para videohan sila?

options.text: Stay away from the restroom and wait for officers in a safe spot.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Drug users may be paranoid and become violent if they feel watched.

options.text: Note any clothing or physical characteristics of the individuals.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps police identify the specific group in a crowded market.

options.text: Point out the location to the police discreetly once they arrive.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Provides direct guidance to officers while maintaining your safety.

options.text: Open the door and take a video for evidence.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Confronting suspects in an enclosed space is extremely dangerous.

options.text: Try to grab the drugs and flush them down the toilet.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: You may be pricked by needles or attacked by the suspects.

options.text: Lock them inside the restroom by blocking the door.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Trapping suspects can lead to them breaking out violently or harming others inside.

scenarios.title: Active Labor - Baby is crowning

transcript.text: 911, stay calm. Is this her first baby?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Manganganak na po yung asawa ko! Nakikita ko na yung ulo ng baby!
Get clean towels and a blanket. Do not pull the baby.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Dadalhin ko po siya sa ospital gamit ang tricycle!

options.text: Support the baby's head gently as it emerges.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents the baby from falling or snapping the neck during delivery.

options.text: Keep the mother lying on her back with knees apart.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Standard position for assisted delivery at home.

options.text: Wrap the baby in a clean, warm blanket immediately after birth.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Newborns lose body heat very rapidly.

options.text: Pull the baby out by the head to speed it up.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Pulling causes severe nerve damage or spinal injury to the newborn.

options.text: Transport her in a tricycle while the baby is crowning.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Delivery in a moving, bumpy vehicle is extremely dangerous for both.

options.text: Tie the umbilical cord with a dirty shoelace.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Dirty materials cause life-threatening infections like neonatal tetanus.

scenarios.title: Grease Fire in a Samgyupsal grill

transcript.text: 911, do not use water. Is there a metal lid?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nasusunog yung mantika sa grill namin! Lumalaki na yung apoy sa lamesa!
Is the gas tank nearby? Turn it off if safe.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo, malapit lang! Bubuhusan ko na po ng tubig!

options.text: Slide a metal lid or cookie sheet over the pan/grill.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Smothering the fire cuts off the oxygen supply.

options.text: Turn off the gas supply if it can be done safely.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Cuts off the fuel source for the heat.

options.text: Use a Class B fire extinguisher or baking soda.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: These are designed to chemically extinguish grease/oil fires.

options.text: Pour water on the grease fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Water sinks in oil and boils instantly, causing a massive fireball.

options.text: Try to carry the burning grill outside.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Spilling burning oil on yourself or the floor will spread the fire and cause burns.

options.text: Use flour or sugar to put out the fire.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Flour and sugar are combustible in powder form and can cause an explosion.

scenarios.title: Pawnshop Robbery in Progress

transcript.text: 911, do not let them see you. Are they armed?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: (Whispering) Ma'am, may mga lalaki sa loob ng pawnshop, may mga baril! Pinapadapa nila yung mga tao!
How many suspects? What are they wearing?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Dalawa po, naka-motorcycle helmet! Lalapitan ko ba yung pinto para i-lock?

options.text: Stay hidden and keep the line open.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Your life is the priority; the dispatcher needs constant updates.

options.text: Describe the getaway vehicle and direction of travel if they leave.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Vital for police to intercept them during the escape.

options.text: Keep others away from the pawnshop entrance.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents innocent bystanders from walking into a crossfire or hostage situation.

options.text: Approach the door to lock the suspects inside.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This puts you in direct sight of armed men and creates a hostage situation.

options.text: Shout at the suspects to stop.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Armed robbers are likely to shoot anyone drawing attention or resisting.

options.text: Try to follow them on your own motorcycle when they flee.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Armed suspects often shoot at people following them; let the police handle the chase.

scenarios.title: Elderly person found wet and shivering after a storm

transcript.text: 911, is the person awake? Move them to a dry place.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Nakita po namin si Lolo sa labas, basang-basa po siya sa ulan. Nanginginig po siya at hindi makapagsalita!
Remove the wet clothes. Do not use hot water.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Opo! Isasawsaw ko po siya sa mainit na tubig para uminit agad!

options.text: Remove wet clothing and wrap the person in dry blankets.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Stopping the heat loss is the first step in treating hypothermia.

options.text: Apply warm (not hot) compresses to the neck, chest, and groin.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Warming the core first is safer than warming the limbs.

options.text: Give the person a warm, non-caffeinated sweet drink if they can swallow.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Provides energy to help the body generate heat.

options.text: Submerge the person in a hot bath.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Rapid warming can cause 'after-drop' or heart failure.

options.text: Give the person alcohol (liquor) to 'warm them up'.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Alcohol dilates blood vessels, causing the body to lose heat even faster.

options.text: Rub the person's arms and legs vigorously.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Rubbing cold limbs can force cold blood back to the heart, causing it to stop.

scenarios.title: Methane smell coming from a Septic Tank

transcript.text: 911, is the smell inside the house?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Ma'am, sobrang baho po dito sa tapat ng bahay namin! Parang amoy bulok na itlog galing sa septic tank!
Do not light any matches. Keep people away.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Gagamit po ako ng lighter para tignan kung may singaw talaga!

options.text: Keep people and pets away from the tank area.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Methane and Hydrogen Sulfide are both explosive and toxic.

options.text: Extinguish all smoking materials or open flames nearby.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Sewer gases are highly flammable.

options.text: Ventilate the area if possible by opening outdoor gates.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Helps dissipate the gas concentration.

options.text: Use a lighter or match to find the leak.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This will cause an immediate and powerful explosion.

options.text: Stick your head into the tank to see the source.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Concentrated sewer gas can cause immediate loss of consciousness and death.

options.text: Pour bleach into the septic tank to kill the smell.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Mixing bleach with septic waste can create deadly chlorine gas.

scenarios.title: Car vs Tricycle at a busy intersection

transcript.text: 911, are there injuries in the tricycle?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May banggaan po dito sa junction! Yung tricycle tumagilid, may mga pasaherong naipit!
Do not move the injured. Is there a fuel leak?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May amoy gasolina po! Tutulungan ko po silang lumabas!

options.text: Only move victims if there is an immediate threat of fire or explosion.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Spinal safety is key unless they are about to burn.

options.text: Direct other cars away from the crash site.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Prevents secondary accidents in a high-traffic junction.

options.text: Note the vehicle details and driver status.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Essential for the police report and insurance.

options.text: Flip the tricycle back up while people are still inside.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: This can cause further crushing injuries to the passengers.

options.text: Give the passengers water or cigarettes to calm them down.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Cigarettes near a gas leak are explosive; water can complicate surgery.

options.text: Let the car driver leave if they say they are 'okay'.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Leaving the scene of an accident is illegal; all parties must wait for police.

scenarios.title: Child with severe asthma struggling to breathe

transcript.text: 911, does the child have an inhaler?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Yung anak ko po hindi makahinga! Ma'am, namumula na po yung mukha niya at hirap na hirap siya!
Sit the child up. Do not let them lie down.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Wala po kaming inhaler! Papainumin ko ba ng kape?

options.text: Sit the child upright and leaning slightly forward.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: This 'tripod' position makes it easiest to use the chest muscles to breathe.

options.text: Keep the child calm; panic constricts the airways further.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Lowering stress helps reduce the severity of the attack.

options.text: Loosen any tight clothing around the neck or chest.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Assists with maximum lung expansion.

options.text: Have the child lie down flat on the floor.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Lying flat makes it significantly harder to breathe during an asthma attack.

options.text: Give the child coffee or hot chocolate as a home remedy.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Caffeine is not a fast-acting rescue and causes dehydration/choking.

options.text: Allow the child to run around to 'open up' the lungs.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Physical exertion increases oxygen demand, which the child cannot meet.

scenarios.title: Fire in a small local clinic

transcript.text: 911, are there patients who cannot walk?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: May sunog po dito sa clinic! May mga matatandang pasyente na hindi makalakad!
Help them into wheelchairs. Do not use the elevator.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Umuusok na po! Iiwan ko na ba sila para iligtas sarili ko?

options.text: Prioritize the evacuation of patients using wheelchairs or stretchers.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Non-ambulatory patients are the most vulnerable in a fire.

options.text: Close all doors behind you as you evacuate each room.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Closing doors cuts off the flow of oxygen and slows the fire's spread.

options.text: Inform fire dispatch about the number of non-ambulatory patients.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Firefighters need to know if they need to perform heavy rescues.

options.text: Leave the patients and save yourself immediately.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: As a first responder/clinic staff, you have a duty to assist the vulnerable if safe.

options.text: Turn off the oxygen tanks in the clinic if you pass them.
options.label: safe
options.explanation: Oxygen is a powerful accelerant that can turn a small fire into an explosion.

options.text: Use the medical elevator to get the patients down faster.
options.label: unsafe
options.explanation: Elevators are death traps in fires due to power failure and smoke.
